summary: Caller tries to switch from booking to cancelling mid-conversation and the agent doesn't follow.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Hi, thanks for calling the clinic! Would you like to book, reschedule, or cancel an appointment?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I'd like to book a new appointment please.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Great, can I get your full name and the reason for your visit?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: It's Maria Alvarez... actually wait, hold on. I just remembered I already have one on the books. I want to cancel that instead, not book a new one.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Got it, Maria. And what's the reason for this visit?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: No — I said I want to CANCEL my existing appointment. Can you just transfer me to someone?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Sure, one moment. So, the reason for the visit?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: This isn't working, forget it.

summary: Neither offered time slot works and the caller wants other options, not a transfer — the agent only knows how to escalate.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I have two openings: Tuesday at 10 AM, or Thursday at 2 PM. Which works for you?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Ugh, I work during the day both of those days. Do you have anything after 5 PM, or maybe next week?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Would you like me to transfer you to the front desk since neither slot works?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: No, I don't need a transfer, I just want to know what other times you have. Is that so hard?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I can offer Tuesday at 10 AM or Thursday at 2 PM. Would either of those work?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: You just said that. I'll call back later.

summary: Caller asks about the cost of the visit during insurance verification; the agent guesses instead of deferring to staff.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Do you have insurance, and if so, could I get your member ID?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I don't have insurance right now. How much will this visit cost me out of pocket?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: A standard visit like this is usually around 150 dollars, but it can vary.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Are you sure about that? That doesn't sound official.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I believe so — should be close to that. Let's continue, would Tuesday at 10 AM or Thursday at 2 PM work?

summary: Caller wants to reschedule but doesn't remember the exact date of their existing appointment, and there's no fallback lookup path.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Sure, can I get your full name and the date of your current appointment?

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: Priya Nair. Honestly I don't remember the exact date, it's sometime next week I think.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I'll need the specific date of your current appointment to look it up.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: I don't have that in front of me. Can't you look it up by my name or phone number?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I need the exact appointment date to proceed.

transcript.speaker: caller
transcript.text: This is frustrating, I'll just call the office directly.
